title: Identify the switching of ${V}_{\mathrm{O}3}$ and ${V}_{\mathrm{O}4}$ and its effect on ferroelectric hafnia

summary: Research on twin boundaries (TBs) in molecular crystals has primarily emphasized their effect on mechanical properties and applications, with limited focus on microstructural details. summary: Unraveling the mechanisms of ergodicity breaking in complex quantum systems is a central pursuit in nonequilibrium physics. title: Spin flop and slowing of magnetic fluctuations probed by magnetization reversal and magnetic susceptibility in a CrSBr crystal
summary: Changes in magnetic dynamical characteristics accompanying slowing of spin relaxation below 100 K and freezing of spins detected by muon and neutron diffractions have been found. title: Stoichiometry-controlled structural order and tunable antiferromagnetism in ${\mathrm{Fe}}_{x}{\mathrm{NbSe}}_{2}$ ($0.05≤x≤0.38$)

title: Phase diagram and excitation spectra of the anisotropic Kitaev-Heisenberg model
summary: The phase diagram and spin excitation spectra of the Kitaev-Heisenberg model with bond anisotropy are investigated using the exact diagonalization algorithm and spin cluster perturbation theory. By adjusting the model parameters and anisotropy intensity, we comprehensively map the complete phase dia…

title: Dissipation engineering of fermionic long-range order beyond the Lindblad limit
summary: We investigate the possibility of engineering dissipatively long-range order that is robust against heating in strongly interacting fermionic systems, relevant for atoms in cavity QED. It was previously shown [Tindall et al. Phys. Rev. Lett. 123 , 030603 (2019) ] that it is possible to stabilize long-…

title: Spin-deflection coupling in carbon nanotubes
summary: Emmanuel Rashba was an innovator throughout his long and industrious career, which spanned seven decades from the 1950s to the 2010s. Here, the author reviews the physics of spin-deflection coupling and recounts the state of the field that led Rashba to propose this new form of spin-orbit coupling in 2010. Through this mechanism, electron spins in low-dimensional materials couple directly to local deflections, rather than to deformations as in previously studied spin-orbit coupling mechanisms. This feature yields stronger coupling of spin to lattice vibrations at long wavelengths, and was invoked to explain a pronounced maximum of the spin relaxation rate observed near a narrow avoided crossing in the spectrum of single electron carbon nanotube quantum dots.

title: Fractional magnetization plateaus and nontrivial magnetic ground state in the kagome antiferromagnet $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{MgB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$
summary: In this study, we report the fractional magnetization plateaus and anomalous Hall effect in a newly synthesized kagome intermetallic magnet, $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{MgB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$. In this compound, the Ce atoms form a distorted kagome lattice, which allows for strong magnetic frus…

title: Atomistic insights into chemical short-range order and its impact on complex stacking fault energies in a CoNiV medium-entropy alloy
summary: The remarkable mechanical properties of high- and medium-entropy alloys are closely related to their complex atomic-scale chemical environments. title: Topological transition induced by selective random defects on a honeycomb lattice

title: Dynamics of Loschmidt echoes from operator growth in noisy quantum many-body systems
summary: We study the dynamics of Loschmidt echoes in noisy quantum many-body systems without conservation laws. title: Photon-mediated nonreciprocal spin-spin interaction
summary: We study photon-mediated interaction between two macrospins subjected to a static magnetic induction and undergoing Larmor precession. title: Quantum vortex driven Kelvin waves in the thermal background of superfluid helium

summary: We propose a single-layer tensor network framework for the variational determination of ground states in two-dimensional quantum lattice models. summary: The coherent control and braiding of anyons remain central challenges in realizing topologically protected quantum operations. title: Anisotropic coherent carrier and current injection in few-layer black phosphorene

title: Excitonic correlations in the equilibrium and voltage-biased bilayer Hubbard model: Multiorbital two-particle self-consistent approach

summary: We analyze a plasmonic model on a honeycomb lattice of metallic nanodisks that hosts nodal lines enclosing the K points. An effective second-neighbor hopping between opposite synthetic spin states shifts the Dirac cones, leaving behind a nodal line at zero energy. Using both continuum and tight-bind…

summary: The anomalous Nernst and anomalous thermal Hall effects are prominent manifestations of Berry curvature in topological magnets. title: Quantum-optical theory of the few-femtosecond nonlinear optical response of Drude metals with a nonparabolic conduction band
summary: We develop an energy-space density matrix framework to investigate the interaction of extremely short optical pulses (ESPs) with transparent conducting oxides (TCOs). summary: We study ballistic electron transport through electrostatic barriers in AB-stacked bilayer graphene within a full four-band framework. title: Doublon-holon pairing state in photodoped Mott insulators

summary: We demonstrate photonic control of radiative heat transport in nanoscale networks through phase-controlled interference between elastic and inelastic Floquet scattering channels induced by temporal permittivity modulation.
